verses.1: The king’s heart is like channels of water in the hand of the LORD; He turns it wherever He pleases.
verses.2: Every person’s way is right in his own eyes, But the LORD examines the hearts.
verses.3: To do righteousness and justice Is preferred by the LORD more than sacrifice.
verses.4: Haughty eyes and a proud heart, The lamp of the wicked, is sin.
verses.5: The plans of the diligent certainly lead to advantage, But everyone who is in a hurry certainly comes to poverty.
verses.6: The acquisition of treasures by a lying tongue Is a fleeting vapor, the pursuit of death.
verses.7: The violence of the wicked will sweep them away, Because they refuse to act with justice.
verses.8: The way of a guilty person is crooked, But as for the pure, his conduct is upright.
verses.9: It is better to live on a corner of a roof Than in a house shared with a contentious woman.
verses.10: The soul of the wicked desires evil; His neighbor is shown no compassion in his eyes.
verses.11: When the scoffer is punished, the naive becomes wise; But when the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
verses.12: The righteous one considers the house of the wicked, Bringing the wicked to ruin.
verses.13: One who shuts his ear to the outcry of the poor Will also call out himself, and not be answered.
verses.14: A gift in secret subdues anger, And a bribe in an inside pocket, strong wrath.
verses.15: The exercise of justice is joy for the righteous, But terror to those who practice injustice.
verses.16: A person who wanders from the way of understanding Will rest in the assembly of the dead.
verses.17: One who loves pleasure will become a poor person; One who loves wine and oil will not become rich.
verses.18: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous, And the treacherous is in the place of the upright.
verses.19: It is better to live in a desert land Than with a contentious and irritating woman.
verses.20: There is precious treasure and oil in the home of the wise, But a foolish person swallows it up.
verses.21: One who pursues righteousness and loyalty Finds life, righteousness, and honor.
verses.22: A wise person scales the city of the mighty And brings down the stronghold in which they trust.
verses.23: One who guards his mouth and his tongue, Guards his soul from troubles.
verses.24: “Proud,” “Arrogant,” “Scoffer,” are his names, One who acts with insolent pride.
verses.25: The desire of the lazy one puts him to death, For his hands refuse to work;
verses.26: All day long he is craving, While the righteous gives and does not hold back.
verses.27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination, How much more when he brings it with evil intent!
verses.28: A false witness will perish, But a person who listens will speak forever.
verses.29: A wicked person displays a bold face, But as for the upright, he makes his way sure.
verses.30: There is no wisdom, no understanding, And no plan against the LORD.
verses.31: The horse is prepared for the day of battle, But the victory belongs to the LORD.
